summary: Authentication implementation follows security best practices. Bcrypt verification via abstraction, exponential lockout with persistence, auto-lock timers, biometric fallback. No vulnerabilities found.

findings.finding: Lockout check happens before any password processing, preventing timing attacks during lockout period.
PasswordHasher interface delegates to BCrypt.checkpw() which is inherently constant-time. No timing oracle possible.
Failure counter and lockout timestamp persisted in EncryptedSharedPreferences (AES-256-GCM). Cannot be tampered with without KeyStore access.
biometricFailureCount is in-memory only (not persisted). App kill resets it to 0. This is acceptable because Android BiometricPrompt has its own hardware-level lockout after multiple failures.
findings.reason: Per-session counter is by design. Hardware handles persistent biometric lockout.

findings.finding: Lockout uses TimeProvider.currentTimeMillis() which can be manipulated with root access. Acceptable threat model for a mobile wallet (root = full device compromise).
findings.reason: If attacker has root, lockout bypass is moot -- they can access storage directly.

findings.finding: Initial lock state is Locked (line 67). App always starts locked, requiring authentication. No bypass path.
